candidates.text:
I regret moving away from Vercel

Sure using a VPS and Coolify is cool 😎

But the number of hours lost to debugging, securing, random crashes, broken connections, docker issues, is absurd

Just not worth it

All services were healthy.

CPU was fine.
Memory was stable.
Health checks were green.

Users were still failing.

- That’s when I understood:

Service health ≠ System health.

Distributed systems don’t fail inside boxes.
They fail in the path between them.

This week I’m diving into observability 
because if you can’t trace a request,
you can’t fix the system.

What do you monitor more:
services or user journeys?
I've been unable to ship my shiny new code through @vercel for the last couple of hours because a datacenter in Dubai suddenly failed. Here I was thinking my build was broken.
All users btw not just some, and fast please, cause my app is legit broken.

Planned to ship 1 of my 4 products today.
Found out Supabase is blocked in India.
Switched auth to Clerk.
Finally ready to deploy.
Vercel: “Deployments are down.”
Universe be like
“Not today” 🥴
